concerns.text: Tasks T020 and T021 implement projection of 'model token embeddings' (internal weights) instead of the 'external frequency distributions' mandated by FR-005 and US-2. This is a semantic weakening of the core methodology, failing to deliver the required 'typological shift' metric derived from corpus statistics.

Task T026a substitutes the 'model-seed null distribution' (FR-004) with a 'Within-Language Baseline' (Llama-3 vs. Mistral). While the plan justifies this as a proxy, the task description does not explicitly document this deviation as a 'staged simplification' or 'approved deviation' in the task text, risking silent constraint relaxation.

Task T029 requires fetching WALS data from a 'pre-verified, checksummed local artifact' but no upstream task exists to acquire, validate, or generate this specific WALS dataset. This creates an unexecutable dependency, violating Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) which mandates raw data acquisition before transformation.

Task T015 calculates 'anisotropy deviation' against a 'reference_value' that is undefined in the spec or plan. SC-001 requires measurement against the 'within-language baseline', but T015 does not explicitly link to this baseline, leaving the calculation logic ambiguous and untraceable.

Tasks T018-T019 describe streaming and processing datasets but lack an explicit task to 'download and checksum' the raw source files first. This violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) which requires checksumming raw data before any transformation.

Task T011a implements a 'deterministic vocabulary mapping layer' but does not specify the strategy (e.g., shared subword dictionary) or fallback behavior beyond 'explicit logging'. The spec's edge case regarding differing vocabulary sizes requires a defined strategy to prevent silent failure, which is currently missing.

Task T008a requires a 'MANDATORY PROFILING' run to verify the 6-hour constraint (SC-005) but fails to define the 'input subset size' or the specific 'threshold' for failure (e.g., 'fail if projected time > 5h'). This makes the 'fail the build' condition subjective and untestable.
